keyFactors: I first chose by color then went by number of successes
strategy: I tried picking based off of % success to failure and then number

keyFactors: The anxiety from a countdown and not immediately understanding the study aims introduced pressure for me, but I eventually setlled on choosing the highest number choice
strategy: At first I chose arbitrarily, because I wasn't quite sure what the 'correct' answer was, eventually I settled on choices with the highest number of successes and failures total, indicating total productivity regardless of success to failure ratio. I wasn't quite sure what the study was asking of me, so I panicked slightly (particularly at the end when allocating numbers)
motivation: Monetary initially; however, I am thoroughly enjoying the variety of experiments I am taking part in, as I have a background in science that makes me curious as to figuring out what a study is truly attempting to measure

keyFactors: The main factors influencing my decision were simply the production numbers. I always picked the one with the highest production, with reasonable amounts of error. This quiz was looking for productivity, not accuracy. If I was looking for complete accuracy, I would be more closely observing success/fail ratios, rather than the raw success count.
strategy: I made sure to check the stats of all the candidates and compared their results to each other. I chose the most productive one among them. When the results were still similar, I simply picked the one I saw first, or the one that had a color I liked (e.g., red).
motivation: Monetary incentive... I would love to do this again for money.

interference: I'm not sure if it was my browser but the applicants were angled vertically and it was blocking an error message in the first trial but I was able to get through all 50. I also missed the instructions due to the orientation on the allocation slide but refreshed the page and was able to finish.
keyFactors: I would try and divide in my head to calculate the odds but when I picked one that had the best odds but didn't get a bonus I switched to only going with what numbers were bigger but had better rations.
strategy: Looking for the highest number of success and if it had an acceptable low number of errors.
motivation: I thought it was cool I was working side by side other participants and that I could earn bonuses.

keyFactors: I just went with my gut.
